title: Dog Inability to Pass Puppy: Emergency Guide for Dystocia
meta_desc: If your dog is unable to pass a puppy during labor, it is a high-priority emergency. Learn the signs of dystocia and when to seek immediate vet care.
content_html:
What is Dystocia?

Dystocia refers to difficult birth or the inability to expel a fetus through the birth canal. This can occur due to maternal factors, such as a narrow pelvis or uterine inertia, or fetal factors, such as a puppy being too large or positioned incorrectly. When a dog is unable to pass a puppy, the lives of both the mother and the unborn litter are at significant risk.

Urgency Level: High Emergency

Inability to pass a puppy is a critical medical emergency. You should contact an emergency veterinarian immediately if you observe any of the following: more than 30 minutes of strong contractions without a birth, more than two hours between puppies, a puppy visibly stuck in the birth canal, or the presence of dark green or bloody discharge before the first puppy is born. Delaying treatment can lead to fetal death or life-threatening complications for the mother.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

If you are communicating with a remote triage vet or preparing to head to the clinic, a clear photo of the mother’s vulva and any visible part of the puppy can be extremely helpful. A photo allows the veterinary team to quickly assess the presentation of the puppy—such as whether it is a breech birth or if there is a physical obstruction—and provide immediate instructions on how to stabilize the dog during transport.

faqs.question: How long can a dog be in labor before it becomes an emergency?
faqs.answer: Active straining with strong contractions should result in a puppy within 20 to 30 minutes. If it exceeds 30 to 60 minutes without a birth, it is an emergency.

faqs.question: Can I pull a stuck puppy out myself?
faqs.answer: You should never pull a puppy forcefully as you can cause internal tearing or fatal injury to the puppy. Only gently assist if specifically instructed by a veterinarian over the phone.

faqs.question: What are the common signs of dog labor distress?
faqs.answer: Signs include extreme lethargy, greenish discharge without a birth, shivering, vomiting, or the mother crying out in pain during contractions.
